mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero average forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicate synchronized demand recovery across durable goods and services, which amplifies equity risk appetite and lifts S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) while single-series coefficients remain positive, indicating no synergistic effect.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding a level of 100 indicates supply-side overheating that triggers input cost inflation and margin compression for S&P 500 constituents, resulting in negative forward equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 100, or the signal exhibits no predictive power beyond a random walk benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP consistently above 2.0 percent reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month forward window.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% correlates with negative or flat S&P 500 forward returns in more than 50% of observations across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings rising above 2.5 percent annually force the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive average forward returns in more than 45% of triggered periods, or the predictive rank correlation drops below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 200,000 and an unemployment rate below 4.5 percent confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that drives consumer demand and equity valuations higher over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline of either series alone in a paired t-test over a 3-year out-of-sample period, indicating the interaction adds no incremental predictive information.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts consistently exceeding 900,000 units annually signal robust construction activity and household formation, which historically precedes broad-based economic expansion and lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution for triggered months shows no statistically significant positive skew (p > 0.05) compared to untriggered months over a 10-year backtest.
